name: dissect-ntfs

metadata.name: dissect.ntfs
metadata.summary: A Dissect module implementing a parser for the NTFS file system, used by the Windows operating system

documentation:
# dissect.ntfs

A Dissect module implementing a parser for the NTFS file system, used by the Windows operating system. For more
information, please see [the documentation](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/projects/dissect.ntfs/index.html).

## Requirements

This project is part of the Dissect framework and requires Python.

Information on the supported Python versions can be found in the Getting Started section of [the documentation](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/index.html#getting-started).

## Installation

`dissect.ntfs` is available on [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/dissect.ntfs/).

```bash
pip install dissect.ntfs
```

This module is also automatically installed if you install the `dissect` package.

## Build and test instructions

This project uses `tox` to build source and wheel distributions. Run the following command from the root folder to build
these:

```bash
tox -e build
```

The build artifacts can be found in the `dist/` directory.

`tox` is also used to run linting and unit tests in a self-contained environment. To run both linting and unit tests
using the default installed Python version, run:

```bash
tox
```

For a more elaborate explanation on how to build and test the project, please see [the
documentation](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/contributing/tooling.html).

## Contributing

The Dissect project encourages any contribution to the codebase. To make your contribution fit into the project, please
refer to [the development guide](https://docs.dissect.tools/en/latest/contributing/developing.html).

## Copyright and license

Dissect is released as open source by Fox-IT (<https://www.fox-it.com>) part of NCC Group Plc
(<https://www.nccgroup.com>).

Developed by the Dissect Team (<dissect@fox-it.com>) and made available at <https://github.com/fox-it/dissect>.

License terms: AGPL3 (<https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html>). For more information, see the LICENSE file.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.c_ntfs.segment_reference
documentation:
Helper to calculate the complete segment number from a cstruct MFT segment reference.

Args:
    reference: A cstruct _MFT_SEGMENT_REFERENCE instance to return the complete segment number of.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.c_ntfs.varint
documentation:
Parse variable integers.

Dataruns in NTFS are stored as a tuple of variable sized integers. The size of each integer is
stored in the first byte, 4 bits for each integer. This logic can be seen in
{func}`AttributeHeader.dataruns <dissect.ntfs.attr.AttributeHeader.dataruns>`.

This function only parses those variable amount of bytes into actual integers. To do that, we
simply pad the bytes to 8 bytes long and parse it as a signed 64 bit integer. We pad with 0xff
if the number is negative and 0x00 otherwise.

Args:

: buf: The byte buffer to parse a varint from.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.c_ntfs.bsf
documentation:
Count the number of trailing zero bits in an integer of a given size.

Args:
    value: The integer to count trailing zero bits in.
    size: Integer size to limit to.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.ntfs.NTFS
documentation:
Implementation for Microsoft NTFS.

This implementation supports parsing NTFS from either a full NTFS volume or from separate files.
If you have a file-like object of an NTFS volume, simply pass it as the fh argument. If you have separate
file-like objects for things like `$BOOT` or `$MFT`, pass those as the boot and mft arguments.
The separate arguments take precedence over parsing from the volume file-like object.

Args:

: fh: A file-like object for the volume to use for parsing NTFS. This is where "data on disk" is read from.
  boot: A file-like object for the `$BOOT` file.
  mft: A file-like object for the `$MFT` file.
  usnjrnl: A file-like object for the `$Extend/$Usnjrnl:$J` file.
  sds: A file-like object for the `$Secure:$SDS` file.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.stream.CompressedRunlistStream
documentation:
Specialized {class}`~dissect.util.stream.RunlistStream` for reading NTFS compressed streams.

Args:

: fh: The source file-like object.
  runlist: The runlist for this stream in block units.
  size: The size of the stream. This can be smaller than the total sum of blocks (to account for slack space).
  compression_unit: The compression unit of for this stream.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.util.apply_fixup
documentation:
Parse and apply fixup data from `MULTI_SECTOR_HEADER` to the given bytes.

Args:

: data: The bytes to fixup

Returns:

: The fixed up bytes.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.util.ensure_volume
documentation:
Check if a volume is available for reading.

A volume in this context refers to a disk or other file that contains the raw NTFS data, not contained
in system files like the `$MFT`.

Raises:

: VolumeNotAvailableError: If a volume is not available.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.util.get_full_path
documentation:
Walk up parent file references to construct a full path.

Args:
    mft: The MFT object to use for looking up file references.
    name: The file name to use.
    parent: The parent reference to start backtracking from.

Raises:
    FilenameNotAvailableError: If an MFT record has no filename.

functions.name: dissect.ntfs.util.ts_to_ns
documentation: Convert Windows timestamps to nanosecond timestamps.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeMap

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeMap.add
documentation:
Add an attribute to the collection.

Note that this is the only intended way to modify the {class}`AttributeMap`!

Args:

: attr: The attribute to add.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeMap.find
documentation:
Find attributes by name and attribute type.

Args:

: name: The name of the attribute to find, usually `""`.
  attr_type: The attribute type to find.

Utility dictionary-like object for interacting with a collection of attributes.

Allows convenient accessing of attributes added to this collection. For example:
\- Get attributes by name, e.g. `attributes.DATA` to get all `$DATA` attributes.
\- Get attributes by type code enum or integer, e.g. `attributes[0x80]` or `attributes[ATTRIBUTE_TYPE_CODE.DATA]`.
\- Check attribute membership by enum or integer, e.g. `0x80 in attributes` or `ATTRIBUTE_TYPE_CODE.DATA in attributes`.
\- Find all attributes with a given name and type, e.g. `attributes.find("$I30", ATTRIBUTE_TYPE_CODE.INDEX_ROOT)`.

Note that any data retrieval from an `AttributeMap` will always succeed and return an
{class}`~dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeCollection`, either empty or containing one or more attributes.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeCollection

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeCollection.open
documentation:
Open the data streams on a list of attributes, resident or non-resident.

Args:
    allocated: Use the actual stream size or the allocated stream size (i.e. include slack space or not).

Returns:
    A file-like object for the data of this list of attributes.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeCollection.size
documentation:
Retrieve the data stream size for this list of attributes.

Args:
    allocated: Return the actual stream size or the allocated stream size (i.e. include slack space or not).

Returns:
    The requested stream size.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeCollection.dataruns
documentation:
Get the dataruns for this list of attributes.

Raises:
    TypeError: If attribute is resident.

Utility list-like object for interacting with a list of attributes.

Allows convenient access to attribute properties for a list of one or more attributes.

For example, if we have only one attribute we want to access the `size`, we want to be able
to do `attribute_list.size` instead of `attribute_list[0].size`.

Additionally, we can also provide functionality here that we want to perform on a group of
attributes, like `open()` and `size()`.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.Secure

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.Secure._iter_entries
documentation:
Iterate over all SDS entries, optionally starting from a specific offset.

Args:
    offset: Optional offset to start iterating from.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.Secure.lookup
documentation:
Lookup a security descriptor by the security ID.

An index is used if available (`$SII`), otherwise we iterate all entries until we find the correct one.

Args:

: security_id: The security ID to lookup.

Raises:

: KeyError: If the security ID can't be found.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.Secure.descriptors
documentation: Return all security descriptors.

Lookup security descriptors from the `$Secure` file, or optionally just a file-like object of the `$SDS`.

Only one the `record` or `sds` arguments needs to be provided.

Args:

: record: The MFT record of the `$Secure` file, used when opening from a full NTFS volume.
  sds: A file-like object of the `$SDS` stream, used when opening from separate system files.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.SecurityDescriptor
documentation:
Parse a security descriptor from a file-like object.

Args:
    fh: The file-like object to parse a security descriptor from.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.ACL
documentation:
Parse an ACL from a file-like object.

Args:
    fh: The file-like object to parse an ACL from.

documentation: Return the ACE type.

documentation: Return whether this ACE is a standard ACE.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.secure.ACE.is_compound_ace

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.usnjrnl.UsnJrnl

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.usnjrnl.UsnJrnl.records
documentation:
Yield all parsed USN records.

Only yields version 2 USN records, other record versions are ignored.

Parse the USN journal from a file-like object of the `$UsnJrnl:$J` stream.

Args:

: fh: A file-like object of the \$UsnJrnl:\$J stream.
  ntfs: An optional {class}`~dissect.ntfs.ntfs.NTFS` class instance, used for resolving file paths.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.usnjrnl.UsnRecord
documentation:
Parse a USN record from a file-like object and offset.

Args:

: usnjrnl: The {class}`UsnJrnl` class this record is parsed from.
  fh: The file-like object to parse a USN record from.
  offset: The offset to parse a USN record at.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.index.Index

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.Index.index_buffer
documentation:
Return the {class}`IndexBuffer` at the specified cluster number.

Args:

: vcn: The virtual cluster number within the index allocation to read.

Raises:

: FileNotFoundError: If this index has no index allocation.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.Index.search
documentation:
Perform a binary search on this index.

Returns the matching node if performing an exact search. Otherwise return the first match that is greater
than the search value.

Args:
    value: The key to search.
    exact: Result must be an exact match.
    cmp: Optional custom comparator function.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: If there is no collation (comparator) function for the collation rule of this index.
    KeyError: If an exact match was requested but not found.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.Index.entries
documentation: Yield all {class}`IndexEntry`'s in this {class}`Index`.

Open an index with he given name on the given MFT record.

Args:
    name: The index to open.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If no index with that name can be found.

documentation: Return the indexed attribute type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexRoot.collation_rule
documentation: Return the collation rule.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexRoot.bytes_per_index_buffer
documentation: Return the size of an index buffer in the index allocation in bytes.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexRoot.clusters_per_index_buffer
documentation: Return the size of an index buffer in the index allocation in clusters.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexRoot.entries
documentation: Yield all {class}`IndexEntry`'s in this {class}`IndexRoot`.

Represents the `$INDEX_ROOT`.

Args:

: index: The {class}`Index`` class instance this {class}`IndexRoot` belongs to.
  fh: The file-like object to parse an index root on.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexBuffer

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexBuffer.entries
documentation: Yield all {class}`IndexEntry`'s in this {class}`IndexBuffer`.

Represent an index buffer in `$INDEX_ALLOCATION`.

Args:

: index: The {class}`Index` class instance this {class}`IndexRoot` belongs to.
  fh: The file-like object of `$INDEX_ALLOCATION`.
  offset: The offset in bytes to the index buffer on the file-like object we want to read.
  size: The size of the index buffer in bytes.

Raises:

: EOFError: If there's not enough data available to read an index buffer.
  BrokenIndexError: If the index buffer doesn't start with the expected magic value.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.dereference
documentation:
Dereference this {class}`IndexEntry` to the MFT record it points to.

Note that the file reference is a union with the data part so only access this if you know the entry has
a file reference and not a data part.

Raises:

: MftNotAvailableError: If no MFT is available.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.key
documentation: Return the index key of this entry.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.data
documentation:
Return the data part of this entry.

Note that the data part is a union with the file reference, so only access this if you know the entry has
data and not a file reference.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.attribute
documentation: Return the {class}`dissect.ntfs.attr.AttributeRecord` of the attribute contained in this entry.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.is_end
documentation: Return whether this entry marks the end.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.is_node
documentation: Return whether this entry is a node.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.node_vcn
documentation: Return the node VCN if this entry is a node.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.length
documentation: Return the length of this index entry.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry.key_length
documentation: Return the length of this index entry.

Parse and interact with index entries.

Args:

: index: The {class}`Index` class instance this {class}`IndexEntry` belongs to.
  fh: The file-like object to parse an index entry on.
  offset: The offset in the file-like object to parse an index entry at.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.Mft

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.Mft.root
documentation: Return the root directory MFT record.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.Mft._get_path
documentation:
Resolve a file path to the correct MFT record.

Args:
    path: The path to resolve.
    root: Optional root record to start resolving from. Useful for relative path lookups.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.Mft.get
documentation:
Retrieve an MFT record using a variety of methods.

Supported references are:
: - `_MFT_SEGMENT_REFERENCE` cstruct structure
  - integer segment number
  - string file path

Args:

: ref: Reference to retrieve the record by.
  root: Optional root record to start resolving from. Useful for relative path lookups.

Raises:

: TypeError: If the reference is of an unsupported type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.Mft.segments
documentation:
Yield all valid MFT records, regardless if they're allocated or not.

Args:

: start: The starting segment number. Use `-1` to start from the last segment.
  end: The ending segment number. Use `-1` to end with the last segment.

Interact with the `$MFT` (Master File Table).

Args:

: fh: A file-like object of the \$MFT file.
  ntfs: An optional NTFS class instance.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.from_fh
documentation:
Parse an MFT record from a file-like object.

Args:
    fh: The file-like object to parse an MFT record from.
    offset: The offset in the file-like object to parse the MFT record from.
    ntfs: An optional NTFS class instance.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.from_bytes
documentation:
Parse an MFT record from bytes.

Args:
    data: The bytes object to parse an MFT record from.
    ntfs: An optional NTFS class instance.

Raises:
    BrokenMftError: If the MFT record signature is invalid.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.get
documentation:
Retrieve a {class}`MftRecord` relative to this one.

Args:

: path: The path to lookup.

Raises:

: MftNotAvailableError: If no MFT is available.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.attributes
documentation:
Parse and return the attributes in this MFT record.

`$ATTRIBUTE_LIST`'s are only parsed if there's an MFT available on the NTFS object.

Raises:

: BrokenMftError: If an error occurred parsing the attributes.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.resident
documentation: Return whether this record's default `$DATA` attribute is resident.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.filename
documentation: Return the first file name, or `None` if this record has no file names.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.filenames
documentation:
Return all file names of this record.

Args:
    ignore_dos: Ignore DOS file name entries.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.full_path
documentation:
Return the first full path, or `None` if this record has no file names.

Args:

: ignore_dos: Ignore DOS file name entries.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.full_paths
documentation:
Return all full paths of this record.

Args:
    ignore_dos: Ignore DOS file name entries.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.is_dir
documentation: Return whether this record is a directory.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.is_file
documentation: Return whether this record is a file.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.is_reparse_point
documentation: Return whether this record is a reparse point.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.is_symlink
documentation: Return whether this record is a symlink reparse point.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.is_mount_point
documentation: Return whether this record is a mount point reparse point.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.reparse_point_name
documentation: Return the (printable) name of this reparse point.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.reparse_point_substitute_name
documentation: Return the substitute name of this reparse point.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.reparse_point_record
documentation:
Resolve a reparse point and return the target record.

Note: absolute links (such as directory junctions) will *always* fail in the context of a single filesystem.
Absolute links include the drive letter, of which we have no knowledge here.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord._get_stream_attributes
documentation:
Return the {class}`~dissect.ntfs.util.AttributeCollection` of all attributes with the given name and attribute type.

Args:

: name: The attribute name, often an empty string.
  attr_type: The attribute type to find.

Raises:

: FileNotFoundError: If there are no attributes with the given name and type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.open
documentation:
Open a stream on the given stream name and type.

Args:
    name: The stream name, an empty string for the "default" data stream.
    attr_type: The attribute type to open a stream on.
    allocated: Whether to use the real stream size or the allocated stream size (i.e. include slack space).

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If there are no attributes with the given name and type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.size
documentation:
Return the stream size of the given stream name and type.

Args:
    name: The stream name, an empty string for the "default" data stream.
    attr_type: The attribute type to find the stream size of.
    allocated: Whether to use the real stream size or the allocated stream size (i.e. include slack space).

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If there are no attributes with the given name and type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.dataruns
documentation:
Return the dataruns of the given stream name and type.

Args:
    name: The stream name, an empty string for the "default" data stream.
    attr_type: The attribute type to get the dataruns of.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: If there are no attributes with the given name and type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.has_stream
documentation: Return whether or not this record has attributes with the given name and type.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.index
documentation:
Open an index on this record.

Args:

: name: The index name to open. For example, `"$I30"`.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.iterdir
documentation:
Yield directory entries of this record.

Args:

: dereference: Determines whether to resolve the {class}`~dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry`'s to {class}`MftRecord`'s. This impacts performance.
  ignore_dos: Ignore DOS file name entries.

Raises:

: NotADirectoryError: If this record is not a directory.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.mft.MftRecord.listdir
documentation:
Return a dictionary of the directory entries of this record.

Args:

: dereference: Determines whether to resolve the {class}`~dissect.ntfs.index.IndexEntry`'s to {class}`MftRecord`'s. This impacts performance.
  ignore_dos: Ignore DOS file name entries.

Raises:

: NotADirectoryError: If this record is not a directory.

MFT record parsing and interaction.

Use the {func}`~MftRecord.from_fh` or {func}`~MftRecord.from_bytes` class methods to instantiate.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.attr.AttributeList

documentation: Iterate all entries within this `$ATTRIBUTE_LIST` and yield all embedded attributes.

classes.name: dissect.ntfs.attr.StandardInformation

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.attr.StandardInformation.creation_time
documentation: Return the `$STANDARD_INFORMATION` `CreationTime`.

methods.name: dissect.ntfs.attr.StandardInformation.creation_time_ns
documentation: Return the `$STANDARD_INFORMATION` `CreationTime` in nanoseconds.
